text: Three trocars are placed in a vertical line along the left rectus abdominis muscle, with the center incision at the umbilicus and additional incisions above and below. The inflamed cecum is visible, complicating the appendectomy procedure, as traditional suturing or stapling methods are deemed unsuitable due to the risk of tissue damage.

The surgeon uses interrupted vicryl sutures to reinforce the closure of the appendiceal stump, preserving the ileocecal valve. Two O-silk sutures are placed at the superior and inferior base of the stump, tying them over the omental patch to approximate the omentum to the inflamed stump.
